title: (Bearish Signal) HLIO, price may drop as it breaks higher Bollinger Band
author: tickeron_community
selftext:
This price move could be a signal that HLIO may fall back below the higher band and head toward the middle band. Traders may consider selling the stock, shorting the stock, or exploring put options. Tickeron's A.I.dvisor backtested 46 similar cases where HLIO's price broke its higher Bollinger Band, and 37 of them led to a successful outcome. Odds of Success: 80%. Current price $58.03 is above $50.63 the highest resistance line found by Tickeron A.I. Throughout the month of 12/03/20 - 01/06/21, the price experienced a +17% Uptrend. During the week of 12/29/20 - 01/06/21, the stock enjoyed a +10% Uptrend growth.

https://preview.redd.it/dyq7k8e1py961.png?width=1529&format=png&auto=webp&s=f6a347a400aa28d42f0554dc19664e0654c899ac

**Bearish Trend Analysis**

The RSI Oscillator demonstrated that the stock has entered the overbought zone. This may point to a price pull-back soon.

The Stochastic Indicator may be shifting from an upward trend to a downward trend. Tickeron A.I. detected that in 45 of 66 cases where HLIO's Stochastic Oscillator exited the overbought zone, the price fell further within the following month. The odds of a continued downward trend are 68%.

Following a 3-day decline, the stock is projected to fall further. Considering past instances where HLIO declined for three days, the price rose further in 50 of 62 cases within the following month. The odds of a continued downward trend are 65%.

HLIO broke above its upper Bollinger Band on January 06, 2021. This could be a sign that the stock is set to drop as the stock moves back below the upper band and toward the middle band. You may want to consider selling the stock or exploring put options.

[Full information about signal here](https://tickeron.com/news/8646488-helios-technologies-s-hlio-57-39-price-may-drop-as-it-breaks-higher-bollinger-band-on-jan-06-2021?utm_source=raddit&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=test&utm_content=article&utm_term=signal)
